column8: Open flow jam - flow as you go\nFunsized, Decoy & friends

In the United States the circumcision of an infant is considered normal. But some people impacted by this action come to view it as a non-consensual genital modification. Circumcision can have negative consequences: reduced sensitivity, curvature of the penis, painful erections and more. Come to this workshop to learn about foreskin restoration. These are methods to grow the skin of the penis with consistent stretching (think ear gauging) to ultimately gain enough tissue for the appearance and functionality of a foreskin. You’ll learn about the history of circumcision in the USA, the anatomy of the penis (do you know where your raphe is?) and about foreskin restoration techniques and resources. I will demonstrate manual stretching exercises and the usage of some commercially available tuggers. The tuggers will be available for people to try out after the lecture. Come with your dick questions.

Let’s open this space with intention and call ourselves home. 

After the flurry of arrival and the energy of setting up camp, join us to drop out of the doing and into the being. 

Through gentle, grounding breath work and shared presence, we will root ourselves into the earth, connect with ourselves, and set a collective intention for the beautiful weekend ahead. 

Welcome home.

People Ranting Enthusiastically About Cultish Hobbies

Got a cool hobby? A scorching hot take? A hill you will die on? Take the mic and testify. P.R.E.A.C.H. is an open soapbox to give amplified sound to the passionate and possessed.

Rants, sermons, TED talks, or interpretive dances welcome. You have three minutes to P.R.E.A.C.H. about whatever your own personal cult is about.

No sign-up, just show up and do your thang. Exceptions apply (aka don't be a dick).

🎨 Face painting

column8:
Temple of the Burning Heart

Rising from the ash on Temple Hill stands—a sanctuary of impermanence, a monument to the stories we leave behind. Its wooden beams whisper with the weight of memory, each carved detail a prayer, each offering a farewell.

Here, sorrow and joy entwine like smoke in the Tennessee wind. Here, burdens are set down, love is spoken, and silence is sacred. Within its radiant glow , past and present embrace—laughter echoes, grief lingers, and forgiveness takes root in the spaces between.

At its heart, fire awaits—not in destruction, but in transformation. Flames will consume, not to erase, but to release. As the temple turns to embers, so do the words, the wounds, the wishes woven into it. Nothing is lost. Everything is carried forward.

This is a place for letting go. This is a place for remembering. This is a place for beginning again.
Join us at Big Fluffy Cloud to make your own open-ended kaleidoscope and see the burn in a whole new way!

column1: The Evolving Edge: Kink, Connection, and the Long-Term Partnership
column8:
Midday Lightly Guided Conversation

When you’ve been together for years, and playing together for just as long, the conversation changes. It’s no longer about discovery in the beginner sense; it’s about evolution.

How do our kinks shift as our bodies change? How do we stay open to new energy when we share domestic life? How do we mediate shared history? 

Join us for a relaxed, lightly facilitated midday honest conversation with other long-term partners.

We hope to go beyond the basics and discuss the nuances of a seasoned kink life:
•The Integration Phase: Moving past scene night and looking at how power, play, and intimacy weave into the fabric of a long-term life.
•The Changing Body: Navigating physical shifts, energy levels, and new boundaries with grace and creativity.
•Deepening the Script: Breaking out of autopilot play and finding new edges within a familiar partnership.
•Emotional Sustainability: Managing the Aftercare of a decade-long relationship.

This is a peer-level space for couples who are already active and looking for a thoughtful,  environment to talk shop. We provide the prompts and the structure; you provide the lived experience.

Expect a friendly, high-trust atmosphere where the goal isn't to fix anything, but to honor where you’ve been and explore where you’re going next.
